title: metastelma tylophoroides
intro: Metastelma tylophoroides, also known as the Purple Allamanda, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. It is a perennial vine with purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Metastelma tylophoroides include treating fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts and leaves are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
